notes: Output {added:ids} with ids=make([]string,len) -> always [] not null (L265-274). Invalid JSON -> exit 2 with decoder detail in hint not error (L60-67). Validation failures -> exit 1 with actionable hint (L210-224). File errors -> exit 3. Success msg to stderr suppressed in JSON mode.

Asserts exit codes 1 (validation) and 2 (usage), hint presence/content, and [] vs null for depends_on/source_sections/tags (L229-263). Also guards structured_elements [] (L265-281) and stats.tags [] (L292-303). errJSON helper parses the {error,code,hint} contract.

auditResult shape stable; checklist/prompts built via make -> [] not null. skipped_roles normalized to [] (L236-241), omitted under --compact (explanatory per §8.4). exitAuditNoFiles -> exit 4 with suggested_files [] surviving compact (L739-745). by_type map sorted by encoding/json. Exit 2/3/4 + hints correct.

Asserts exit 4 with suggested_files as JSON array (L90-107), empty -> [] (L111-123), type-filtered (L128-145), and surviving --compact (L149-161). Covers --affected-from-tasks exit 0 (L166-178) and flag-conflict exit 2 (L199-213). Hint names --affected-files/--affected-from-tasks.

Summary map keys stable; loadAuditMergeRows (L124) and dedupAuditRows (L172) return make([]...) -> [] not null. overlap_report omitted under --compact (L85, explanatory). Deterministic: dedup sorts by (role,item_id) (L183-192). Exit 2 (no args/spec positional) and 3 (missing file) correct.

Asserts merged_count/duplicates_removed/findings counts and by_status map (L17-54). Empty and malformed-only inputs succeed exit 0 with merged_count 0 and empty -o file (L59-103). No-args exit 2 (L107), missing file exit 3 (L116), spec positional rejected exit 2 unparsed (L128-142).

latestAuditRoundOverlapReport returns []engine.RoleOverlap{} explicitly on every empty path (L45,49,53) -- explicit nil-guard, never null. computeAuditOverlapReport clusters via an order slice preserving first-seen insertion order; PASS rows excluded; delegates deterministic sort to engine.OverlapReport.

Asserts §3.1 body order (role<rules<excerpt<checklist<files<schema), checklist embedded as JSON array with item_id and expected_evidence (never empty), spec excerpt only in spec-coverage, CLAUDE.md excerpt only in maintainability, and byte-identical deterministic regeneration (L69-94).

runStatus: audit_rounds -> [] not null (L161 init). max_rounds/rounds_remaining null when uncapped, present+survive --compact when capped (L184-195, decision-critical per §8.4). in_flight_round int when >0 else null (L198-202). overlap_report omitted under compact. Exit 3 (file) / 1 (invalid JSON) / 4 (--check not converged) correct; defaultHint fills gaps.

ChecklistItem is all-scalar (item_id/type/spec_line/section/text/expected_evidence), no omitempty gaps. auditPrompt.checklist_items always [] (built via make, generateRoleAuditPrompts L174). affected_files never null: engine.SelectAuditFiles returns make([]AuditFileEntry,0) on every empty path (auditfiles.go:125,158,178) and AuditFileEntry.Tasks=[]string{}.

Doc comments on decodeTaskJSON(72-74), computeAccuracyRatio(127-128), addTasks(151-161); consistent output.Error+os.Exit+return nil pattern; no TODO/FIXME; var tasks []model.Task(100) is internal accumulator not JSON-serialized; addTasks locked-write closure is cohesive with section comments.

All test helpers documented (initEntryProject 23, errJSON 36, readRawTaskFile 220); consistent TestAdd_* naming; t.Helper() on all helpers; nil-slice regression tests (229-263) guard the var-[]T-to-JSON contract.

runAudit confirmed ~98 lines post-refactor (extracted determineAuditFiles 254-278 + loadAuditSpec 284-324, both with doc comments); exported ChecklistItem(37) has doc comment(35-36); all JSON-output slices use make([]T,0); no TODO/FIXME; errors consistent.

All test helpers documented (gitOut 16, commitFile 27, writeTaskFileRaw 36, newAuditRepo 44, doneTaskJSON 56, closeTaskFile 79); consistent exit-code + hint assertions; toStringSlice(68) is package-private helper.

Doc comments on runAuditMerge(14-18), loadAuditMergeRows(111-115), dedupAuditRows(167-169); all JSON slices initialized make([]T,0)(124,172); 1MB scanner buffer vs readFindings 64KB is intentional (audit notes can be long); no TODO/FIXME.
All 6 test functions documented with §-references; consistent exit-code + stderr-content assertions; covers dedup, empty, malformed, no-input, missing-file, spec-positional cases.

Both functions documented (computeAuditOverlapReport 9-15, latestAuditRoundOverlapReport 40-42); reuses shared engine.OverlapReport and parseNDJSONFile(review_report.go:169) — no duplication; functions 38 and 13 lines; clusterKey type-local.
Integration tests verify §3.1 body order + §3.2 JSON embedding + §3.4 role-specific sections + deterministic regeneration; findItemEvidence(96) uses t.Helper(); consistent assertion style.

Doc comments on all 4 functions(15,85,116,138); countAuditFindings wraps error with %w(128) — correct propagation; runAuditStatus(140-212)=73 lines within tolerance; rounds:=[]ReviewRound{}(161) avoids null; mechanize_candidates(18) is correct field name.

Doc comments on every function(26,59,86,103,117,169,200,222,252,278,297); buildRolePrompt(119-167)=49 lines; generateRoleAuditPrompts(173-198)=26 lines; consistent id-prefix + role-const patterns; no TODO/FIXME; no dead code.

readBulkTasks: os.ReadFile err->ExitFile, json.Unmarshal err->ExitUsage, scanner.Err()->stderr warn (over-long line). All write paths via engine.WithFileLock; addTasks validates resolvable ids + NormalizeSourceSections err->exit. No unchecked scanner or silently-dropped parse/read error.

TestAdd_BulkWarnsOnOverlongLine directly exercises the scanner.Err() warning path (70KB line -> exit 0 + stderr 'over-long line'/'dropped', t2 dropped). All fixtures use require.NoError + 0o600 + filepath.Join; nil-slice->[] contract also asserted (L229-263).

taskAcceptanceEntries (ReadFile err: IsNotExist->nil else warn; json.Unmarshal->warn) + readFindings (ReadFile IsNotExist->nil else exit; json.Unmarshal->warn; scanner.Err->warn L661). execGitDiff L462-481 also checks scanner.Err (L477). No nil-slice leak to JSON: filterChecklistByType/buildChecklist use make([]T,0).

exit-4 contract + suggested_files=[] (not null) verified; helpers use require.NoError on all WriteFile/git ops + 0o600 + filepath.Join. toStringSlice uses guarded _,_ type-assert (safe zero fallback). No swallowed error.

loadAuditMergeRows: Stat IsNotExist->exit, Open err->exit, json.Unmarshal->warn, incomplete row->warn, scanner.Err()->warn (L158). Output WriteFile 0o600 (L90); json.Marshal err->warn+continue (L48). Rows init make([]map,0) so empty->[] not null. Clean.
TestAuditMerge_OnlyMalformedSucceeds verifies warn-on-malformed + warn-on-incomplete paths (exit 0). All WriteFile require.NoError + 0o600 + filepath.Join. No unchecked error.

latestAuditRoundOverlapReport: parseNDJSONFile err->documented empty-slice return (L52-53); path built via filepath.Join (L51); returns []engine.RoleOverlap{} (not nil) so no null leak. computeAuditOverlapReport uses make([]T,0). No read/parse of user data, no lock needed (pure derived).

All fixture writes use require.NoError + 0o600 + filepath.Join; runTP results checked via require.Equal(code). Type-assertions are post-shape-checks on known JSON. No swallowed error or null-slice risk in test assertions.

recordAuditRoundEntry writes round file + state under engine.WithReviewStateLock, WriteFile 0o600 via filepath.Join (L99), writeErr/loadErr/SaveReviewState propagated. countAuditFindings json.Unmarshal->%w error return (L128); no scanner. os.ReadFile/Stat/SpecHash errors all exit. ComputeRolesHash best-effort (empty hash triggers staleness, not data loss).

claudeMDExcerptFor: exec.Output err->skip candidate, os.ReadFile err->continue (documented best-effort); auditDiffStats/auditDeletedFiles: exec err->empty map (documented). json.Marshal(item) at L138 is a known-safe all-string/int struct (cannot fail). Paths via filepath.Join. No task-file writes here (prompt builder only).
loadMergeFindings no longer aborts on an all-empty input set; an empty/clean round yields zero findings and exits 0 (runReviewMerge L15-104). Mirrored by runAuditMerge (audit_merge.go L19-109).
foldClosureCommit amends C1 to fold the closure (guards in canAmendClosure L262-281, followUp fallback L285-291). Exercised by close_checkpoint_test.go (commit + done --auto-commit + fallback).

ValidateTaskEntry enforces id/title/acceptance/anchor/unknown-deps; addTasks (add.go L210-225) calls it at entry with ExitValidation(1) and ExitUsage(2) for invalid JSON. Coverage auto-filled (add.go L255-257).
The converged (empty) round is the best-tested merge path: empty inputs exit 0 with merged_count 0 (review_merge.go L116-152, audit_merge.go L116-165). Principle realized in code.

commit.go stages the wip task file into C1 (L117-129), records C1 (L144), writes closure, then foldClosureCommit leaves the working tree clean for tp-owned paths. TestCloseCheckpoint_CommitFoldsClosure confirms git status clean.
addTasks validates every task before append via ValidateTaskEntry (entry.go) + intra-batch dup check; bad tasks never reach the file. add_validation_test.go covers all 7 rules.

skipped_roles names every non-emitted role (review.go L424-432, audit.go L234-241); attribution_excludes surfaces regression exclusion (review_overlap.go L69-84); coverage-unreadable-spec finding names the path (validate.go L92-101).
Execute() routes flagUsageError→ExitUsage(2), LockTimeoutError→ExitState(4), else ExitValidation(1). Exit constants 0/1/2/3/4 honored throughout (exitcode_conformance_test.go).

lock_timeout_seconds is config-resolved (effectiveLockTimeoutSeconds) and surfaces as max_rounds/rounds_remaining/in_flight_round in --status, each replacing an agent inspection rather than adding a manual knob.
BuildNextAction switches over the existing PhaseReview/PhaseAudit/PhaseImplement/PhaseDecompose/PhaseRelease set only; no new phase or convergence-criteria change introduced in v0.29.0.

Runtime fan-out / injection-duty / permission-prompt guidance lives in SKILL.md (L161-167) and README.md (L398-402), never branched on inside Go code paths (no opencode/runtime detection in internal/).

resolveUsageForm/resolveAllUsageForm state '0-based index'; runReviewResolve validates the index as an integer before the disposition (L59-67) so a non-numeric index reports the expected form, not 'invalid status'.

tp never invokes hc: under hc, tp commit / done --auto-commit are rejected (commit.go L44-48, done.go L127-138) and a close requires --commit/--covered-by; no code path exec's hc or sweeps uncommitted state.
TestReviewMergeAllEmptyFilesCreateEmptyOutput (exit 0 + empty -o), TestReviewMergeMissingFile (exit 3), TestReviewMergeZeroFiles (exit 2) cover all three exit branches of §3.

Prompt emission lists every non-emitted role in skipped_roles (review.go L424-432, audit.go L234-241); --merge and --status add attribution_excludes only when it caused merged_count>overlap count (review_overlap.go L69-84). Tests L106-216 cover both + --compact omission.

computeAuditOverlapReport clusters NON-PASS rows by (item_id, category) (L16-38); emitted by --merge (audit_merge.go L85-87) and --status (audit_record.go L178-180). TestAuditMerge_OverlapReport + role_attribution_transparency_test.go L219-288.

--status reports max_rounds (null when uncapped, L75-86), rounds_remaining, and in_flight_round (L89-93); a snapshot without a round file makes tp resume point at recording it (resumepayload.go L150-156). loop_budget_inflight_test.go covers review+audit+--compact.

suggestFilesFromTasks unions paths touched by every SHA in commit_shas of done tasks, type-filtered (L705-731); exitAuditNoFiles emits them at exit 4 with a hint naming --affected-files/--affected-from-tasks (L739-745); determineAuditFiles routes --affected-from-tasks (L254-264).

WithFileLockTimeout loops TryLock with doubling backoff (10ms→50ms cap) until timeoutSeconds elapse, returning *LockTimeoutError; root.go Execute (L169-173) maps it to exit 4 with a hint naming the lock path + elapsed wait. TestWithFileLockTimeout_HeldPastTimeout (lock_test.go L84).

Each §13.1 row has a test asserting the required exit code AND a non-empty hint: invalid-JSON add (exit 2), flag-parse failure (exit 2), dash-leading done/commit reason (exit 2, hint names '--'), plus state/usage errors carry hints (§13.2).

When roundedActual==0 the accuracy is null with note "duration below resolution" and excluded++ (L129-132); summary excludes null-accuracy tasks from estimation_accuracy via accEstimated/accActual (L175-178) and reports excluded_from_accuracy. TestReportAllExcludedAccuracy (report_test.go L635).

CheckEmptySections skips a container heading whose next heading is deeper (L36-38) but still fires empty-section on an empty leaf (L39-44). TestCheckEmptySections (lint_test.go L58) 'container heading with deeper child not flagged' + 'empty leaf' cases.

TestProjectCommitStrategySettableAndResolved: set --workflow --project commit_strategy=hc writes .tp/config.json and config --resolved names source 'project'; TestTaskLevelCommitStrategyRefusalNamesProjectSetter: task-level set still exits 2 with a hint naming the project setter. runSetProjectWorkflow (set_project.go L45-54) + set.go L316-318.

Verified live: go build ./... clean, golangci-lint run → 0 issues, go test ./... all pass. SKILL.md L161-167 + README.md L398-402 + CLAUDE.md L235 carry the §17 non-Claude-runtime / injection-duty / permission-prompt guidance; REFERENCE.md present.

--merge now produces an empty -o file on a clean round (L77-87); tp review <spec> --record <empty> already counts the round as clean (§3.4), so the merge→record chain is reachable with no manual file creation.

isSpecLookingPath rejects a .md/.markdown positional in --merge (review_merge.go L25-34, audit_merge.go L29-38) and --resolve (review_resolve.go L48-55), each exit 2 naming the expected form. TestAuditMerge_RejectsSpecPositionalExit2 + TestReviewMerge_RejectsSpecPositionalExit2.

TestCloseCheckpoint_DoneAutoCommitFoldsClosure asserts committed task file shows status:done + working tree clean; TestCloseCheckpoint_FallbackDirtyNonTpPath asserts the amend guard falls back to chore(tp): record <id> closure when a non-tp path is dirty.

TestResume_Bookkeeping_ClosureForClosedTaskFile: under hc the dirty task file appears in bookkeeping (kind=closure, ref=task id), NOT in changes, and raises no unexplained-changes blocker. DeriveBookkeeping in engine/bookkeeping.go.

LockFilePath returns .tp/locks/<base>-<hash>.lock (L115-124); WithFileLock removes a stale sibling <path>.lock on acquisition (L63-67) and removes the lock file on release (L93-96); gitStage refuses any *.tasks.json.lock --files entry (commit.go L202-204).

TestAdd_Rejects* covers each §6.1 row (blank id, dup, blank title/acceptance, missing anchor, unknown dep, invalid JSON) with the stated exit + hint; add_validation_test.go L242/L262/L302 assert every slice field serializes as [] not null.

addTasks recomputes AutoFillCoverage after append when the spec is readable (L255-257); the engine/coverage_write_test.go + coverage_validate_test.go confirm init+add+validate is clean with no import round trip, and validate.go L92-101 hints the unreadable spec path when it cannot.

ExtractSpecExcerptForTask falls back to extractSectionsExcerpt (heading + body to next same/shallower heading, listed order, blank-line joined) capped at maxExcerptChars=2000 (L12). plan.go/show.go/next.go all call it; TestSpecExcerptParity_SectionsOnly (v2_commands_test.go L402) covers plan/show/next/next --peek + --compact omission.
runReviewMerge clusters (L170-203) and emits NDJSON; merged_count=len(unique)>0, exit 0. TestReviewMergeTwoFilesDedup + TestReviewMergeOverlapReport.
outputPath!="" writes the (empty) ndjson via os.WriteFile and summary reports merged_count 0; exit 0. TestReviewMergeAllEmptyFilesCreateEmptyOutput (review_merge_test.go L260-299).

malformed (invalid JSON) and incomplete (missing location/severity/finding) lines are skipped with a stderr warning naming which; exit stays 0, empty output. TestReviewMergeAllInvalid + TestAuditMerge_OnlyMalformedSucceeds.
loadMergeFindings stats each path; os.IsNotExist → output.Error(ExitFile) exit 3 before any output is written. TestReviewMergeMissingFile + TestAuditMerge_MissingFileExit3.
os.Open failure on an unreadable file → output.Error(ExitFile) exit 3; since the loop exits on first failure, all-unreadable also exits 3 with no output file written.
len(args)==0 → output.Error(ExitUsage) exit 2. TestReviewMergeZeroFiles (review_merge_test.go L155) + TestAuditMerge_NoInputFilesExit2.
ValidateTaskEntry returns id-blank when TrimSpace(ID)==""; hint names the field. addTasks maps it to ExitValidation(1). TestAdd_RejectsBlankID.

addTasks tracks existing ids incrementally (file + intra-batch) and rejects a duplicate with ExitValidation(1); hint names the existing task. TestAdd_RejectsDuplicateID + TestAdd_BulkRejectsIntraBatchDuplicate.
TrimSpace(Title)=="" → title-blank finding, hint names the field, ExitValidation(1). TestAdd_RejectsBlankTitle.
TrimSpace(Acceptance)=="" → acceptance-blank, hint names the field, ExitValidation(1). TestAdd_RejectsBlankAcceptance.
len(SourceSections)==0 && TrimSpace(SourceLines)=="" → source-anchor finding; hint names BOTH anchors. ExitValidation(1). TestAdd_RejectsMissingSourceAnchor.

ValidateTaskEntry collects every depends_on id absent from resolvableIDs and returns unknown-dependency with a hint listing the ids; ExitValidation(1). Resolvable set includes intra-batch ids (add.go L196-202). TestAdd_RejectsUnknownDependency.

decodeTaskJSON failure → output.Error(ExitUsage, "invalid task JSON", err.Error()): decoder detail in hint, not error; exit 2. Bulk path mirrors (L109-115). TestAdd_RejectsInvalidJSON_UsageWithDecoderInHint.
Was exit 3 with raw decoder in error; now ExitUsage(2) with "invalid task JSON" and decoder detail in hint. TestExitCode_AddInvalidJSON_Exit2_DecoderInHint asserts code=2 and decoder only in hint.

wrapFlagErrors wraps cobra flag errors as flagUsageError; root.go Execute (L159-166) emits the tp {error,code,hint} object at exit 2. TestExitCode_FlagParseFailure_Exit2_TPErrorObject + Subcommand variant.

usageErrorDetail detects a dash-leading positional on done/close/commit via dashLeadingPositional and sets a hint naming the '--' separator; exit 2. TestExitCode_DoneDashReason_Exit2_HintNamesSeparator + commit/after-commit-flag variants.

determineAuditFiles: --affected-from-tasks audits suggestFilesFromTasks output directly (L255-264); when no audit-able file is found, exitAuditNoFiles (L739-745) emits suggested_files (union of done-task commit_shas paths, type-filtered, [] when none) at exit 4 with a hint naming --affected-files/--affected-from-tasks. suggested_files emitted directly, so survives --compact.

commit.go: stage wip task file into C1 (L117-129), commit→C1 (L135), record SetCommitSHAs([C1]) (L144), write closure, foldClosureCommit (L156). commit_sha records the pre-amend C1 (L142-144 note); canAmendClosure guards HEAD==C1 and working-tree==tp-written paths only (L262-281), else followUpClosureCommit 'chore(tp): record <id> closure' (L285-291). done.go L245-305 mirrors for --auto-commit. Tests in close_checkpoint_test.go + close_checkpoint_internal_test.go.

runSetProjectWorkflow validates commit_strategy ∈ {builtin,auto,hc} (L46-54) and writes workflow.commit_strategy into .tp/config.json (L135-138), participating in resolve-at-read order; config --resolved annotates its source layer (strategy_config_test.go L97-104 source='project'); task-level set still exits 2 naming the project setter (set.go L316-318, strategy_config_test.go L115-123).

addTasks calls AutoFillCoverage after append when specExists (L255-257); import/remove/set source_* call it too (engine/coverage_write_test.go). When the spec is unreadable the block is left untouched and validate.go L92-101 surfaces a coverage warning whose Hint names the unreadable spec path.

SKILL.md documents non-Claude-runtime driving (sub-agent fan-out L165-167, orchestrator injection duty L161, permission-prompt rule L166); README.md L398-402 + CLAUDE.md L235 mirror it; REFERENCE.md present. Verified live: go build clean, golangci-lint 0 issues, go test ./... all pass.

ValidateTaskEntry enforces rules 1,3,4,5,6 (id/title/acceptance/anchor/unknown-deps); add.go handles rule 2 (dup) and rule 7 (invalid JSON→exit 2). Slice fields serialize [] not null: stats.go L87-90 (tags), add_validation_test.go L242-302 (depends_on/source_sections/tags + lint structured_elements + stats tags).

ExtractSpecExcerptForTask dispatches to extractSectionsExcerpt when sourceLines is empty (L133-141): each resolved section contributes heading+body through the line before the next same/shallower heading, in listed order, joined by blank line, capped at maxExcerptChars=2000 with marker. plan.go L208, show.go L84, next.go L212 call it; --compact omits spec_excerpt (v2_commands_test.go L449-457).

Each §13.1 case returns exit 2: invalid-JSON add with decoder in hint (add.go L60-67), cobra flag-parse failure as tp error object (usage_error.go + root.go L159-166), dash-leading done reason with hint naming '--' (usage_error.go L45-55). Every non-zero error object carries a hint naming a next command (exitcode_conformance_test.go L86-102).

CheckEmptySections uses HeadingContentRange; skips when end>=start OR when the next heading is deeper (container, L36-38); fires empty-section only for an empty leaf (L39-44). TestCheckEmptySections (lint_test.go L58-97): 'container heading with deeper child not flagged' + 'empty leaf heading followed by sibling' + 'empty leaf as last heading'.

WithFileLockTimeout retries TryLock with exponential backoff (lockBackoffStep=10ms→lockBackoffMax=50ms) until effectiveLockTimeoutSeconds (default 5, range 1-60 via workflowFieldRange set.go L419-420) elapse, then returns *LockTimeoutError → exit 4 + hint. config.go/config_extract.go/set.go/set_project.go thread the resolved timeout through writes.

LockFilePath (L115-124) → .tp/locks/<base>-<hash>.lock; EnsureTPGitignore writes a 'locks/' entry (projectconfig.go L324-350) and is called by tp init (init.go L97-101); stale sibling <base>.tasks.json.lock removed on first write (L63-67); commit.go gitStage refuses *.tasks.json.lock (L202-204).

--status writes snapshot-round-N.md atomically at round start (loadAuditSpec/loadReviewSpec via WriteSnapshotAtomic) and review-round-N.ndjson at record; reports max_rounds (null uncapped, L75-86), rounds_remaining, in_flight_round (L89-93); resume next_action=record-round N (resumepayload.go L150-156, L166-172); regression names snapshot-round-<N-1>.md and round 1 skips regression no-baseline (review.go L750). loop_budget_inflight_test.go covers review/audit/compact.

loadMergeFindings aborts ONLY on a missing/unreadable file (exit 3); all-empty and malformed/incomplete lines are skipped with stderr warnings without changing the exit code, so merged_count is 0 and exit is 0. Mirrored by loadAuditMergeRows (audit_merge.go L116-165). TestReviewMergeAllEmptyFilesCreateEmptyOutput + TestAuditMerge_EmptyInputSucceeds.

--merge rejects a spec-looking positional among inputs with exit 2 (review_merge.go L25-34, audit_merge.go L29-38); --resolve/--resolve-all take the findings NDJSON as positional and reject a .md positional exit 2 (review_resolve.go L48-55, L167-174); indices are 0-based and stated in usage (L22-27); a non-numeric index exits 2 (L59-67).

actual_minutes rounded to 1 decimal (roundTo L183-186); roundedActual==0 → accuracy null + note 'duration below resolution' + excluded++ (L127-132); summary excludes them from estimation_accuracy (accEstimated/accActual, L175-178) and reports excluded_from_accuracy; note omitted under --compact (runReport L69-73). TestReportAllExcludedAccuracy.

DeriveBookkeeping classifies dirty tp-owned paths into closure/round/config with {path,kind,ref} (ref = task id / round number from filename / path basename), separate from changes; closureEntries derives one entry per task whose managed fields changed since HEAD (L108+). resume.go L113-137 emits bookkeeping + guidance; both survive --compact (L113-115, L134-136). resume_bookkeeping_test.go covers all kinds + empty + guidance + compact.

overlapReportWithAttribution lists 'regression' in attribution_excludes only when a regression-only cluster caused merged_count>overlap-report count (L69-84); skipped_roles carries {role,reason} with reason in {no-checklist-items,no-spec-change,domain-mismatch,no-baseline} (review.go L703-750, audit_roles.go L192, skipped.go L46); audit overlap_report over non-PASS rows clustered by (item_id,category) (audit_overlap.go L16-38). All three omitted under --compact; role_attribution_transparency_test.go L106-288.
